item.text:
I found a solid open-source set for this. The strongest pattern is: use a stateful agent framework, add a long-term memory layer, run a local inference stack tuned for consumer GPUs, and bolt on a realtime voice layer.

**Best candidates by focus**

1. Letta  
Persistent/stateful agents with explicit long-term memory and “learn/self-improve over time” positioning.  
https://github.com/letta-ai/letta

2. LangGraph  
Good for durable, long-running agents with persistence, resumability, and memory across sessions.  
https://github.com/langchain-ai/langgraph

3. Mem0  
A memory layer for agents with long-term personalization and retrieval.  
https://github.com/mem0ai/mem0

4. LangMem  
Focused on agents learning from interactions via memory extraction, consolidation, and prompt refinement.  
https://github.com/langchain-ai/langmem

5. AutoGen  
One of the strongest open-source frameworks for multi-agent conversations and orchestration.  
https://github.com/microsoft/autogen

6. CrewAI  
Popular multi-agent orchestration framework for role-based agent teams.  
https://github.com/crewAIInc/crewAI

7. CAMEL  
Research-heavy multi-agent framework with statefulness and large-scale agent coordination.  
https://github.com/camel-ai/camel

8. MetaGPT  
Multi-agent “AI software company” approach; useful if your agent needs specialized sub-roles.  
https://github.com/FoundationAgents/MetaGPT

9. OpenHands  
Open-source software-development agent that can edit code, run commands, browse, and operate tools.  
https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands

10. Aider  
Very practical self-editing/code-modification layer for repo-local improvement loops.  
https://github.com/Aider-AI/aider

11. Continue  
Open-source coding agent stack with CLI/headless/background agent workflows.  
https://github.com/continuedev/continue

12. Voyager  
Important reference for lifelong learning, skill libraries, iterative self-improvement, and self-verification.  
https://github.com/MineDojo/Voyager

13. AutoGPT  
Still relevant as a continuous-agent platform and benchmark/tooling ecosystem.  
https://github.com/Significant-Gravitas/AutoGPT

14. llama.cpp  
Best low-level local inference building block for quantized models on consumer hardware.  
https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp

15. ExLlamaV2  
Especially relevant for RTX 4070-class GPUs; optimized for local inference on consumer NVIDIA cards.  
https://github.com/turboderp-org/exllamav2

16. LocalAI  
OpenAI-compatible local serving layer for text, audio, and multimodal components.  
https://github.com/mudler/LocalAI

17. LiveKit Agents  
Strong open-source framework for realtime voice/multimodal agents with telephony/WebRTC.  
https://github.com/livekit/agents

18. Pipecat  
Very good for voice-first multimodal agent pipelines with STT/TTS/LLM orchestration.  
https://github.com/pipecat-ai/pipecat

19. Intervo  
Open-source conversational voice platform for more complete voice-agent products.  
https://github.com/Intervo/Intervo

**Best fit for your specific goals**

If you want an autonomous self-improving agent, I’d shortlist this stack:

- Persistent core: Letta or LangGraph
- Memory: Mem0 or LangMem
- Self-editing/code improvement: OpenHands + Aider
- Multi-agent coordination: AutoGen or CrewAI
- Local RTX 4070 inference: ExLlamaV2 + llama.cpp
- Voice/audio: LiveKit Agents or Pipecat

**Best RTX 4070 picks**
Inference from project docs: for a 12 GB RTX 4070, the most practical local stack is usually `ExLlamaV2` for EXL2/GPTQ-style quantized models, `llama.cpp` for GGUF flexibility, and `LocalAI` if you want an OpenAI-compatible local endpoint.

If you want, I can turn this into a tighter recommendation matrix next:
“best for coding agent,” “best for memory-heavy agent,” “best all-local stack,” and “best voice-enabled stack.”
